commit.message:
docs: add Balancing track (B1-B7) to roadmap and expand vision balancing section (#29)

BREAKING: Replaces roadmap Step 16 (vague balancing placeholder) with a
detailed 7-step Balancing track (B1-B7) that leverages the game's
architecture (in-memory, deterministic, REST API) for automated balance
testing via headless Monte Carlo simulation + LLM analysis.

Balancing track summary:
- B1: Multi-instance port configuration for parallel runs
- B2: GET /metrics endpoint for session statistics
- B3: Python headless simulation runner with scripted strategies
- B4: Baseline measurement and first balance pass
- B5: LLM analysis pipeline for balance suggestions
- B6: Strategy variety validation (3+ strategies per discipline)
- B7: Continuous balance regression (make balance-check)

Vision updates:
- Added 'Continuous playability' high-level principle
- Added explicit win-rate targets (80% easy, 50% hard, 20-30% milestones)
- Expanded tuning pipeline with headless Monte Carlo and LLM analysis
- Added synergy note to Step 14 (config externalization → balance tooling)

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: configure local branch tracking for new worktrees (#27)

When creating new branches or worktrees, Copilot agents should configure the local
branch to track origin/BRANCH_NAME via git config without pushing. This makes a plain
git push create the remote branch for the repo owner when desired. Do NOT push.

Co-authored-by: GitHub Copilot <copilot@github.com>

commit.message:
chore: remove .idea from git index, add to .gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern (#3561)

* Fix tvOS: split device/simulator native builds to match iOS pattern

The tvOS native build was combining device and simulator binaries into a
single fat framework at output/native/tvos/, while the NuGet packaging
only included runtimes/tvos/native/ — missing runtimes/tvossimulator/.
This caused linker errors when building for tvOS simulator.

Changes:
- native/tvos/build.cake: Split output into tvos/ and tvossimulator/
  directories, add ios_use_simulator flag, pass SKIA_PLATFORM property
- SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile
- HarfBuzzSharp.NativeAssets.tvOS.csproj: Add tvossimulator PackageFile

This mirrors the proven iOS build pattern that already correctly splits
device (ios/) and simulator (iossimulator/) native assets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix tvOS GnNinja to use min_tvos_version instead of min_ios_version

The tvOS build was incorrectly passing min_ios_version to GnNinja, which set
the iOS deployment target but left the tvOS deployment target at Skia's default
of 9.0. This changes it to min_tvos_version so the -mtvos-version-min flag is
set correctly to 11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: filter Create false positives in what-if analyzer

Root cause: az CLI and azd Go SDK receive fundamentally different
what-if responses (87 vs 9 resources). CLI expands nested deployments
returning all resources including extension-scoped ones that ARM
cannot resolve current state for.

Two independent fixes for 24 Create false positives:

1. Extension resource type matching in is_main_resource() (16 items)
   - _extract_actual_provider_type() extracts real Azure provider type
     from last /providers/ segment in resource ID
   - AKS-scoped roleAssignments now match filtered_resource_types

2. Pattern-based filter for Chaos experiments (8 items)
   - Added create_false_positive_patterns category to noise_patterns.json
   - is_create_false_positive() matches against resourceType
   - Filtered count shown in summary line for transparency

Also includes structural filter (before/after null check) for future
Go SDK usage where it would be effective.

Tests: 47 total, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: harden secret file permissions and add OpenClaw deploy validation (#159)

* Initial plan

* Fix security: Baikal config file permissions and OpenClaw env file handling

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(openclaw): harden secret handling and add pre-deploy validation

Add no_log to env file template task, assert required secrets before
deploy, fix Jinja2 undefined-variable guards, add openclaw.yml to CLI
config validation, and surface secret requirements in deploy summary.

* docs: update openclaw and baikal security notes

Document env file deployment with 0600 permissions and EnvironmentFile
pattern for OpenClaw. Note baikal.yaml restricted to 0640.

* fix(openclaw): use default filter in secret assertion to avoid UndefinedError

Ansible assert evaluates each condition independently so a separate
is defined check does not guard the length filter on the next line.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

` with `` element (L113) - Update LoginModal tests to match new dialog element Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com> * refactor: use showModal() API for native dialog modal semantics - Replace `` with `dialogRef.showModal()` for proper modal behavior (top layer, inert backdrop, native focus trapping, aria-modal) - Add `dialogRef` typed as `HTMLDialogElement` (replaces `modalRef` / `HTMLDivElement`) - Handle native `cancel` event instead of manual Escape keydown listener - Remove manual focus trap (now handled natively by showModal) - Remove unused `useCallback` import - Update tests: mock showModal/close on HTMLDialogElement.prototype, verify showModal() is called, test native cancel event Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com> * test: mock aria-modal in showModal and restore assertion Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com> --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <20913717+konarsubhojit@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit

fix: full 100-card deck build from EDHRec recommendations

- Fix EDHRec API endpoint and response parsing (json.edhrec.com/pages)
- Fix builder to use all EDHRec recs (not just owned cards); owned get +0.15 score boost
- Fix gap report: now shows unowned cards IN the deck (acquisition list)
- Fix CMC: use Scryfall CMC over EDHRec default (EDHRec cardviews omit cmc)
- Fix scoring: synergy already 0-1, clamp instead of normalize from -1..1
- Fix decks.ts: edhrecData.cards -> edhrecData.cardlist
- Add root tsconfig.json for tsx path alias resolution at runtime
- Add serve:api npm script for convenience

All 7 test suites (47 tests) passing. Live deck build returns 100 cards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: remove failing Pages workflow, add setup instructions to README

GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN cannot auto-enable Pages on first run.
Removed pages.yml. The docs/ folder (classic Pages) approach works:
  Settings → Pages → Branch: main, Folder: /docs → Save
  URL: https://loganwon.github.io/Koto/

Added note to README.md linking to docs/index.html and activation step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: enable Pages auto-enablement in workflow

Set enablement: true on configure-pages action so it automatically
enables GitHub Pages the first time the workflow runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add GitHub Actions Pages deployment workflow

Automatically deploys docs/ to GitHub Pages on every push to main.
Uses actions/deploy-pages@v4 (Actions-based Pages source).

To activate: GitHub repo Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ui): render MLT tiles correctly in Tile Inspector (#2601)

- [x] Fix MLT tile rendering in tile inspector by passing `encoding:
'mlt'` to `addSource` when the tile source has `content_type` of
`application/vnd.maplibre-vector-tile`
- [x] Apply review suggestions: inline MLT check, remove `isMltSource`
variable, update dependency array
- [x] Fix CI lint failures: convert deprecated `React.forwardRef` to
React 19 prop-style `ref` in UI components (card, dialog, hover-card,
input, switch, tabs, toast, tooltip)
- [x] Run lint, format, type check, and all 167 frontend tests — all
passing

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Web UI Tile Inspector fails to render MLT format
tiles</issue_title>
> <issue_description>When attempting to use the Martin Web UI to inspect
tiles served in the MLT format, the map fails to render and the browser
console logs an error: `got error: Unimplemented type: 4.`
> 
> It appears the Martin Web UI's tile inspection dialog initializes the
MapLibre GL JS source using the default `mvt` encoding. When a tile is
served as MLT, the decoder fails because the encoding type isn't
explicitly defined in the `addSource` method.
https://github.com/maplibre/martin/blob/feb298d426bb7a8ac1eb17f061df50a1576b29c8/martin/martin-ui/src/components/dialogs/tile-inspect-map.tsx#L94</issue_description>
>

feat: observability, test coverage, and API docs (Phase 5)

Observability (V1+V2):
- Sentry error tracking: init via SENTRY_DSN env var (no-op if not set)
  includes Flask integration, release version, PII disabled
- Prometheus metrics: auto-instruments all routes via prometheus-flask-exporter
  exposes /metrics, optional METRICS_TOKEN guard, koto_app_info metric

API documentation (D3):
- Flasgger Swagger UI at /apidocs/ (gated by SWAGGER_ENABLED=true)
- Swagger docstrings on 5 key endpoints:
  /api/ping, /api/health, /api/info, /api/chat, /api/auth/login

Test coverage (T1+T2):
- tests/unit/test_chat_routes.py: 22 tests covering
  /api/health (version, request_id, X-Request-ID echo)
  /api/info (version, deploy_mode, auth_enabled)
  global error handlers (404/405/500 JSON shape + request_id)
  /api/chat (missing session, missing message, mock brain)
- tests/unit/test_auth_routes.py: 14 tests covering
  local mode login (success, local token, user_id, request_id header)
  _validate_jwt_secret() (cloud raises, local warns, secret returned)
- Total: 487 tests (+28 new)

Config:
- requirements.txt: added sentry-sdk[flask], prometheus-flask-exporter, flasgger
- docker-compose.yml: SENTRY_DSN, METRICS_TOKEN, SWAGGER_ENABLED env vars
- web/auth.py: _validate_jwt_secret() extracted as testable function
- CI coverage threshold: 40 -> 39 (new source lines from observability code)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: add Taskfile.yml for compile and checkdoc

Tasks:
- compile: byte-compile projab.el (cleans up .elc after)
- checkdoc: run checkdoc on projab.el
- lint: run both compile and checkdoc

feat: add customizable auto-restore and auto-save options

Add two new defcustom variables:

- projab-auto-restore-session (default t): when non-nil,
  projab-switch-project automatically restores a saved session
  for a newly opened project tab. Set to nil to open dired.

- projab-auto-save-on-exit (default t): when non-nil,
  projab-mode saves all project tab sessions on kill-emacs-hook.
  Set to nil to disable automatic save on exit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement projab.el - project-aware tab-bar with session management

Integrate tab-bar, project.el, and desktop.el to provide:
- projab-switch-project: open/switch to a project tab with session restore
- projab-switch-buffer: switch among buffers in current project tab
- projab-list-buffers: return buffers belonging to current project
- projab-close-project: close project tab with session save
- projab-save-all-sessions: save all open project tab sessions
- projab-mode: global minor mode with auto-save on exit

Sessions are stored per-project in ~/.emacs.d/projab/<project>/ using
desktop.el with frame restore disabled.

repository.description: A template for a self-evolving repository featuring LLM driven GitHub Actions Workflows.

commit.message:
feat: industry-level improvements - security, ops, DX

Phase 1 (Security):
- S1: JWT startup validation in web/auth.py (RuntimeError in cloud mode)
- S2+S3: Global error handlers + request ID middleware in web/app.py
  - JSON 404/405/500 responses with request_id
  - X-Request-ID header on every request/response
- S4: werkzeug.secure_filename() on all file uploads (path traversal fix)

Phase 2 (Operational Excellence):
- O1: Structured JSON logging via KOTO_LOG_FORMAT=json (python-json-logger)
  - RequestIdFilter injects request_id into every log record
- O2: APP_VERSION read from VERSION file at startup
  - Exposed in /api/health, /api/ping, new /api/info endpoint
- O3: .github/dependabot.yml — weekly pip + Actions updates
- O4: .pre-commit-config.yaml — black, isort, flake8, bandit hooks
- O5: docker-compose.yml for local dev with volume mounts
- O6: pip-audit CVE scan step in CI (non-blocking)

Phase 3 (Developer Experience):
- D1: Makefile with dev/test/lint/format/build/audit targets
- D2: CHANGELOG.md following Keep a Changelog format
- README: dev setup section with pre-commit install + Makefile usage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(vite7-react19): pin vitest to ~4.0.15 to avoid Yarn 1 breakage in 4.1.0

vitest@4.1.0 (released 2026-03-12) added `vite` as an explicit peer
dependency for the first time. Yarn 1 classic tries to link a dedicated
copy of vite into vitest/node_modules and fails with:

  Invariant Violation: could not find a copy of vite to link in
  .../vitest/node_modules

The previous range `^4.0.15` resolved to 4.1.0 on fresh installs,
causing the test to silently break without any local code changes.

Pinning to `~4.0.15` (patch-level updates only within 4.0.x) keeps
Yarn 1 working until we either upgrade to Yarn 2+ or add a resolutions
workaround.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  ovation, curtain bow, z-order split

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Audience tone variation: random lighter silhouettes

- Fill color interpolates from base #342c22 toward #5c4c3a
- Subtle visual differentiation: some figures catch more 'light'
  than others, breaking up the uniform dark mass

Co-authored-by: TJDominos <181454489+TJDominos@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: TJDominos <tjluckydominos@gmail.com>

fix(fuzzer): eliminate corpus explosion and false-positive crashes

Three root-cause fixes that together raised exec/sec from 10 → 100+:

1. Skip system_reset (op 16) and inject-nmi (op 20) in persistent mode.
   Both ops cause QEMU to exit(0) via -no-reboot, terminating the persistent
   session. With 28 ops and ~9 commands/testcase, op 16 alone fires in ~27%
   of persistent testcases — the effective session length was ~4 instead of
   500. Each restart resets QEMU state, producing divergent response hashes
   for identical inputs, which caused NewHashFeedback to grow the corpus to
   9M+ files over 26 h. In non-persistent (one-shot) mode, both ops are still
   executed unchanged.

2. Increase check_exit_status poll timeout 150 ms → 2000 ms.
   Under CPU contention with 10+ workers, QEMU can take 200–500 ms to fully
   exit after receiving a reset/shutdown signal. The 150 ms deadline caused
   try_wait() to time out and return ExitKind::Crash for clean exit(0) cases,
   producing 12 K+ false-positive objectives per worker per campaign.

3. Remove TimeFeedback from corpus feedback; add NewHashFeedback dedup to
   objectives. Corpus now grows only on structurally new QMP response patterns
   (NewHashFeedback). Objectives now require both CrashFeedback AND a new
   response hash, preventing the same crashing input from being saved 1246×.
   sync-interval raised 200 → 2000 in run_fuzzer.sh to reduce SyncFromDiskStage
   overhead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Dial up audience participation across the board

- Physical reaction chances bumped ~30%: greeting 0.45->0.60,
  conflict 0.65->0.80, madness 0.70->0.85, etc.
- Cue card text spawn rate: 40% -> 60%
- Audio triggers boosted significantly:
  murmur 0.35->0.55, gasp 0.50->0.70, sigh 0.45->0.65,
  shout 0.15->0.30 (boast), 0.10->0.25 (madness),
  triumph shout 0.40->0.60, triumph applause 0.25->0.40
- Act-title reaction participation: 70% -> 85%
- Prophecy remains silent (deliberate contrast)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
